what_it_does: React overlay component that displays a live roster of sessions, lets the operator filter and select one, then mounts a DNA-rendered session card for the selected session alongside recall-lens notes (open loops and recent items).
observations: It delegates the session-card rendering entirely to a global window.DNA pipeline rather than rendering a local JSX card.
It keeps a local, synchronous filter over title/name/cwd/state and caps the displayed matches at 60.
It opens on a custom window 'sessions:open' event and uses Escape to back out of a drill then close the overlay.
It fetches /dna/layouts.json once to load the 'session-card' archetype and caches it on DNA._sessionCardArchetype.
It degrades with plain text messages if DNA, the adapter, or the archetype is unavailable.
It never shows the raw session id as the title and uses ARIA dialog attributes for the overlay.
standouts: DNA archetype mounting via faceRecord.sessionRecord + renderArchetype.
Client-side roster filter with the 60-item cap applied to filtered matches.
Recall-lens UI surfacing open_loops and catch_up items.
conventions: Follows React hooks and a store-driven state model via sessionDrillStore.
Follows the overlay pattern with a scrim click-to-close and Escape handling.
Breaks typical React composition by injecting DOM produced by an external DNA renderer into a React ref.
Uses window custom events for opening the overlay.
Encodes design constraints in comments, e.g. from-DNA law and operator-law.
concerns: The card-mounting effect depends on [selected, sessions], so it re-runs whenever the sessions list updates even if the selected row has not changed.
Window.DNA access is repeated through a helper cast rather than a stable typed reference.
Archetype loading is hard-wired to /dna/layouts.json and the 'session-card' key.
Recall-lens list items use array index as React key.
No explicit TypeScript interfaces for session rows or lens item shapes are visible in this file.
notes: A host-level React overlay that lists sessions, filters them by human-readable fields, and hands off the drilled-in card to a DNA archetype renderer while showing recall lenses.
questions: What is the exact runtime shape of a session row returned by useSessions?
What transform does DNA.faceRecord.sessionRecord perform and what record shape does the session-card archetype expect?
What is the full contract for lens items beyond a text field?
Which component or system dispatches the 'sessions:open' window event?
What visual/styling contract does sessions.css provide for the card host and lenses?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements a session roster overlay with filtering, drill-down, DNA card mounting, and recall-lens display as described in its header comment.
apparent_themes: Render through shared DNA archetypes rather than bespoke host components.
Graceful degradation when external rendering services are unavailable.
Live supervisory overlay for ongoing sessions.
intention_signals: Build a lightweight supervisor surface where a human can quickly locate a session by name, folder, or state, open it to see a DNA-designed session card, and get contextual recall without the host owning card rendering.
novelty: The combination of a React host overlay with client-side filtering and a global DNA archetype renderer that injects the session card DOM is distinct from a conventional self-contained React component.
contribution: It contributes a host seam that connects the session store, a DNA archetype rendering pipeline, and recall-lens presentation, adding roster filtering, keyboard navigation, and clean degradation while leaving the card rendering to DNA.
summary_for_embedding: SessionDrill.tsx is a React overlay that lists live sessions, filters them by human-readable fields, drills into a selected session, and mounts its card through a global DNA archetype renderer with recall-lens panels.
intention_for_embedding: Provide an operator-facing session supervisor that finds sessions by meaning and delegates the detailed card view to DNA archetypes.

symbols.does: Describes the expected shape of the global window.DNA object used for archetype rendering.
symbols.description: A local TypeScript type that declares optional DNA methods and cached state: faceRecord.sessionRecord for adapting a raw row, renderArchetype for producing DOM, and _sessionCardArchetype for caching the loaded layout.

symbols.does: Returns the optional window.DNA cast to the DNAGlobal type.
symbols.description: A small accessor function that repeatedly casts window to an object containing DNA so the component can call the DNA rendering pipeline without importing it.

symbols.does: Renders the sessions overlay, handles open/close/drill interactions, filters the roster, and mounts the DNA session card.
symbols.description: The main React component that consumes sessionDrillStore state, registers window event listeners for opening and Escape, manages a local filter, renders the filtered roster capped at 60 items, and mounts the DNA-rendered card plus recall lenses when a session is selected.

symbols.does: Resets the roster filter and opens the sessions overlay.
symbols.description: An event handler attached to the 'sessions:open' window event; it clears the local filter string and calls openSessions from the store.

symbols.does: Handles Escape by backing out of a drilled session or closing the overlay.
symbols.description: A keydown listener that, when Escape is pressed and the overlay is open, deselects the current session if one is selected; otherwise it closes the overlay.
